findings.summary: networkx declared in optional group but not imported anywhere in infrastructure
evidence: pyproject.toml line 66: graph = [ 'networkx>=3.0' ] declared as optional dep group
Grep of 'networkx' across infrastructure/ returns zero matches — no infrastructure module imports it
The only potential consumers are in projects/cognitive_case_diagrams (untracked output directory), not infrastructure
findings.suggestion: Remove networkx from the infrastructure pyproject.toml optional group or move it to the cognitive_case_diagrams project-level pyproject.toml if that project actually requires it

findings.summary: infrastructure/llm/review/metrics.py has 5 importers but zero direct tests
evidence: Blind packet critical_untested list: { 'file': 'infrastructure/llm/review/metrics.py', 'importers': 5 }
File contains ReviewMetrics, ManuscriptMetrics, SessionMetrics, StreamingMetrics dataclasses plus estimate_tokens() — all business logic with no corresponding test file
estimate_tokens() implements a fixed approximation (len(text) // 4) with no boundary tests
findings.suggestion: Add tests/infra_tests/llm/test_metrics.py covering each dataclass field, estimate_tokens boundary values (empty string, multi-byte chars), and StreamingMetrics arithmetic

findings.summary: infrastructure/publishing/citations.py has 4 importers but no direct tests
evidence: Blind packet critical_untested list: { 'file': 'infrastructure/publishing/citations.py', 'importers': 4 }

Module contains format_authors_apa, format_authors_mla, generate_citation_bibtex, generate_citation_apa, generate_citation_mla — all string-formatting logic with multiple branch conditions (1 author, 2 authors, 3+ authors)
format_authors_apa and format_authors_mla share identical branch structure; divergent edge-cases (empty list, unicode names) are untested
findings.suggestion: Add tests/infra_tests/publishing/test_citations.py with parametrized tests across 0, 1, 2, and 3+ author cases for all three citation styles, and bibtex output structure validation

findings.summary: infrastructure/llm/templates/base.py and manuscript.py have 3 importers each and no direct tests
evidence: Blind packet critical_untested: 'infrastructure/llm/templates/base.py' (3 importers), 'infrastructure/llm/templates/manuscript.py' (3 importers)
base.py ResearchTemplate.render() raises LLMTemplateError on missing variables — error path is untested
manuscript.py REVIEW_MIN_WORDS dict is duplicated verbatim at lines 34 and 55; no test catches this regression
findings.suggestion: Add tests for ResearchTemplate.render() with complete and missing variable sets; add a test that asserts len(set) of REVIEW_MIN_WORDS keys to catch the duplicate-definition regression

findings.summary: projects/code_project/scripts/optimization_analysis.py is 1662 LOC with zero importers and zero test coverage
evidence: Blind packet existing_findings: 'orphaned: Orphaned file (1662 LOC): zero importers, not an entry point'
Blind packet: 'test_coverage: Untested critical module (1662 LOC, 0 importers) — high blast radius'
No test file for this script exists in projects/code_project/tests/ per directory_profiles
findings.suggestion: Extract algorithmic functions from optimization_analysis.py into projects/code_project/src/optimizer.py (thin orchestrator pattern) and cover them through the existing test_optimizer.py suite

findings.summary: infrastructure/publishing/metadata.py has 3 importers and no direct tests
evidence: Blind packet critical_untested: { 'file': 'infrastructure/publishing/metadata.py', 'importers': 3 }

extract_publication_metadata() reads markdown files and applies regex heuristics for author extraction — untestable without a real file; no test exercises the happy path or the exception path (line 43: except Exception)
findings.suggestion: Add a parametrized test with tmp_path markdown fixtures covering title extraction, author extraction, and the fallback-to-defaults path when file content does not match

findings.summary: REVIEW_MIN_WORDS dict is defined twice verbatim in the same file
evidence: Lines 34-40: REVIEW_MIN_WORDS = { 'executive_summary': 250, 'quality_review': 300, ... }
Lines 55-61: identical REVIEW_MIN_WORDS dict definition with identical values and identical inline comments
The second definition silently overwrites the first; any change to one is not reflected in the other
findings.suggestion: Remove the second definition (lines 55-61). The constant should appear exactly once; the repeat suggests a paste-duplicate from an LLM generation session

findings.summary: logging_utils.py contains docstrings that fully restate function names as their only description on trivial helpers
evidence: log_header() docstring: 'Log a section header with visual emphasis' — implementation is four logger.info() calls

log_substep() docstring: 'Log a substep within a stage with consistent indentation. Adds a leading newline and indentation for visual separation.' — body is a single logger.info(f'\n  {message}') call
_resolve_logger() has a docstring ('Return logger if provided, otherwise create a module-level fallback') that reproduces the one-liner body verbatim
Matches 'Docstring bloat: multi-line docstrings on trivial 2-line functions' pattern from ai_generated_debt dimension
findings.suggestion: Condense docstrings on sub-5-line helpers to a single sentence or remove them; reserve multi-line docstrings for functions with non-obvious behavior

findings.summary: log_operation context manager logs both 'Starting: X' and 'Completed: X' on every call, creating noisy entry/exit traces
evidence: logging_utils.py line 246: logger.log(level, f'Starting: {operation}')
logging_utils.py line 254: logger.log(level, f'Completed: {operation} ({duration:.1f}s)')
log_function_call decorator (line 343) separately logs 'Calling: {func.__name__}' and 'Completed: {func.__name__}' — a second entry/exit layer
Blind packet god_module excerpt confirms 110 importers; every caller gets entry+exit log lines, producing double-entry noise when log_function_call and log_operation are both in scope
findings.suggestion: Move 'Starting:' log to DEBUG level by default and only emit 'Completed:' when duration exceeds the threshold; the current behavior matches 'Nosy debug logging: entry/exit logs on every function'

findings.summary: LogAggregator class inside logging_utils.py is an unreachable dead surface that duplicates reporting module concerns
evidence: logging_utils.py lines 646-845: LogAggregator, collect_log_statistics, generate_log_summary defined in the logging utility module
LogAggregator is not exported in __all__ (line 502-541); it is not imported by any file in the import graph
infrastructure/reporting/ already contains test_reporter.py and test_summary_generator.py for log/report aggregation — identical purpose
Matches 'Pass-through wrapper functions with no added logic' and generic-name pattern (handleData / processItem equivalent)
findings.suggestion: Remove LogAggregator, collect_log_statistics, and generate_log_summary from logging_utils.py; if log aggregation is needed, implement it in infrastructure/reporting/ where it belongs

findings.summary: infrastructure/core/ contains 27 files mixing 5+ distinct concerns with no internal subpackage boundaries
evidence: directory_profiles: infrastructure/core/ has file_count=27, total_loc=9644 — exceeds 10-file flat-directory threshold

Files span: pipeline orchestration (pipeline.py, pipeline_summary.py, multi_project.py), performance profiling (performance.py, performance_monitor.py — two separate files), security (security.py, credentials.py), menu UI (menu.py), logging (logging_utils.py + 4 logging_*.py split files), environment (environment.py), config (config_loader.py, config_cli.py)

performance.py and performance_monitor.py both expose profiling dataclasses; __init__.py re-exports PerformanceMonitor from performance.py AND CodeProfiler from performance_monitor.py as if they are equivalent
findings.suggestion: Extract at minimum: pipeline/ subpackage (pipeline.py, pipeline_summary.py, multi_project.py, checkpoint.py), security/ subpackage (security.py, credentials.py), and logging/ subpackage (logging_utils.py + logging_constants.py + logging_formatters.py + logging_helpers.py + logging_progress.py); collapse performance.py and performance_monitor.py into one module

findings.summary: infrastructure/reporting/ zone classification marks test_reporter.py and test_summary_generator.py as 'test' zone inside a production package
evidence: directory_profiles for infrastructure/reporting/: zones = { 'production': 10, 'test': 2 } — two files classified as test-zone inside the production reporting package
test_reporter.py and test_summary_generator.py live in infrastructure/reporting/ not in tests/infra_tests/reporting/, breaking the test-mirrors-production layout used everywhere else
tests/infra_tests/reporting/ has test_test_reporter.py — a test file that tests a file already named test_reporter.py in a production package, creating a confusing naming chain
findings.suggestion: Rename infrastructure/reporting/test_reporter.py to infrastructure/reporting/coverage_reporter.py and infrastructure/reporting/test_summary_generator.py to infrastructure/reporting/summary_generator.py; move any test-only logic to tests/infra_tests/reporting/

findings.summary: infrastructure/validation/validate_markdown_cli.py mutates sys.path at module level inside a library package
evidence: validate_markdown_cli.py lines 21-22: repo_root = Path(__file__).parent.parent; sys.path.insert(0, str(repo_root))
This mutation fires at import time for any caller that imports from this module, not just when the script is run as __main__
Blind packet existing_findings for scripts/01_run_tests.py flags 'sys.path mutation at import time (boundary purity leak)'; the same defect exists inside the infrastructure package itself
findings.suggestion: Guard sys.path manipulation with 'if __name__ == "__main__"' or remove it entirely since the package is already on sys.path via pytest configuration (pythonpath in pyproject.toml)

findings.summary: infrastructure/core/ contains two parallel performance modules with overlapping dataclass hierarchies
evidence: performance.py defines: ResourceUsage, PerformanceMetrics, PerformanceMonitor, get_system_resources, performance_context
performance_monitor.py defines: ProfilingMetrics, CodeProfiler, benchmark_function, benchmark_llm_query, profile_function, get_performance_monitor
Both are re-exported side-by-side in infrastructure/core/__init__.py lines 110-119 without any grouping comment explaining their distinct roles

infrastructure/core/__init__.py exposes PerformanceMonitor (from performance.py) AND get_performance_monitor (from performance_monitor.py) — two different objects for what appears to be the same concern
findings.suggestion: Merge performance.py and performance_monitor.py into a single infrastructure/core/performance.py; if the profiling concern is large enough, extract to infrastructure/profiling/ as a separate subpackage
